creator: bhughes@freebsd.org
text:
Hi Yuri :)

My motivation is essentially the same as in comment #2: packages should be as functional as possible, even if it means more dependencies. These client library dependencies are not that large:

New packages to be INSTALLED:
	turnserver: 4.5.0.7
	postgresql95-client: 9.5.10
	mysql56-client: 5.6.38_1
	libedit: 3.1.20170329_2,1
	liblz4: 1.8.0,1
	hiredis: 0.13.3

Number of packages to be installed: 6

The process will require 53 MiB more space.
4 MiB to be downloaded.

At $dayjob, we are using the Redis statsdb functionality to report individual TURN session usage across our global TURN network, which is something I would be very reluctant to disable by default. Now that flavors have landed in the ports tree, perhaps a "lite" version which disables everything but SQLite would be a better idea?
